title: Listening - Part 3: Technology at Home
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a new piece of technology they use at home. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main benefit each speaker mentions. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a new piece of technology they use at home. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main benefit each speaker mentions. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about using a new piece of technology at home.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: Our heating used to operate on the same schedule every day, even when the house was empty because we were away or working late. I knew that wasn't sensible, but changing settings was awkward. [19]The new smart control lets us turn the heating down from our phones and shows exactly how much energy we are using, so we avoid warming empty rooms unnecessarily.[/19] Our bills have fallen a little, which is welcome, though I mainly like knowing that we aren't wasting energy without noticing.||Speaker 2 Man: I adopted a cat who needs small meals throughout the day. On office days I used to leave a large amount of food out, but she ate it immediately and then became hungry later. [20]An automatic feeder now opens at set times while I'm away, meaning she gets appropriate portions throughout the day even when I cannot be at home to feed her myself.[/20] She learned the sound of it opening remarkably quickly. It has also stopped me worrying about her whenever trains delay my journey home.||Speaker 3 Woman: There was a series of bicycle thefts from gardens near our house, and I became nervous whenever I went away overnight. We didn't want a complicated alarm system, but we installed a doorbell camera. [21]It sends an alert if someone approaches the front entrance and allows us to see who is outside, which makes me feel the house and anything stored beside it are better protected.[/21] Most alerts are only delivery drivers or neighbourhood cats, but it is reassuring to be able to check.||Speaker 4 Man: I used to vacuum the kitchen and living room almost every day because our dog leaves hair everywhere. It wasn't difficult, but it was a task I never looked forward to after work. [22]The small robot vacuum now moves around those rooms while I'm out, so I come home to clean floors without having spent my evening doing the same routine job.[/22] It cannot manage stairs or corners perfectly, and the dog was suspicious at first, but it has given me back a surprising amount of time.||Speaker 5 Woman: My parents live abroad and aren't confident using laptops or complicated apps. We used to arrange phone calls, but they wanted to see their granddaughter growing up. I bought them a simple screen designed for video calling. [23]With one touch, they can now see and speak to us regularly, so distance no longer prevents them from sharing small family moments such as birthdays or my daughter's drawings.[/23] It isn't the same as visiting, obviously, but my daughter now recognises them immediately instead of treating them like strangers.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: Our heating used to operate on the same schedule every day, even when the house was empty because we were away or working late. I knew that wasn't sensible, but changing settings was awkward. The new smart control lets us turn the heating down from our phones and shows exactly how much energy we are using, so we avoid warming empty rooms unnecessarily. Our bills have fallen a little, which is welcome, though I mainly like knowing that we aren't wasting energy without noticing.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: I adopted a cat who needs small meals throughout the day. On office days I used to leave a large amount of food out, but she ate it immediately and then became hungry later. An automatic feeder now opens at set times while I'm away, meaning she gets appropriate portions throughout the day even when I cannot be at home to feed her myself. She learned the sound of it opening remarkably quickly. It has also stopped me worrying about her whenever trains delay my journey home.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: There was a series of bicycle thefts from gardens near our house, and I became nervous whenever I went away overnight. We didn't want a complicated alarm system, but we installed a doorbell camera. It sends an alert if someone approaches the front entrance and allows us to see who is outside, which makes me feel the house and anything stored beside it are better protected. Most alerts are only delivery drivers or neighbourhood cats, but it is reassuring to be able to check.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I used to vacuum the kitchen and living room almost every day because our dog leaves hair everywhere. It wasn't difficult, but it was a task I never looked forward to after work. The small robot vacuum now moves around those rooms while I'm out, so I come home to clean floors without having spent my evening doing the same routine job. It cannot manage stairs or corners perfectly, and the dog was suspicious at first, but it has given me back a surprising amount of time.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: My parents live abroad and aren't confident using laptops or complicated apps. We used to arrange phone calls, but they wanted to see their granddaughter growing up. I bought them a simple screen designed for video calling. With one touch, they can now see and speak to us regularly, so distance no longer prevents them from sharing small family moments such as birthdays or my daughter's drawings. It isn't the same as visiting, obviously, but my daughter now recognises them immediately instead of treating them like strangers.

options.text: It saves them time on a daily task.
It helps them use less energy.
It keeps their home more secure.
It helps them care for a pet.
It allows them to keep in touch.
It makes it easier to sleep.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The smart heating control enables the household to reduce wasted energy (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The automatic feeder allows the speaker's cat to receive correctly timed meals while she is out (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The camera provides alerts and visibility that make the speaker feel the home is safer (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The robot vacuum removes a frequent household task from the speaker's evening routine (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The easy video-calling screen improves contact between the speaker's family and distant parents (evidence [23]).
